title: RingCentral Events API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and terminology for the RingCentral Events (formerly Hopin) platform.

terms.definition: A top-level account entity in RingCentral Events that owns events, templates, and settings. Corresponds to a company or team using the platform.
A virtual, hybrid, or in-person event hosted on the RingCentral Events platform. Events contain sessions, stages, booths, registrations, and tickets.
A record of an attendee signing up for an event. Contains attendee details, ticket assignment, and check-in status.
A ticket type for an event that defines pricing, capacity, and access permissions. Attendees hold tickets when registered.
A virtual expo booth within an event where sponsors or exhibitors can showcase products and engage with attendees.
A scheduled breakout session or workshop within an event. Sessions have speakers, start/end times, and may have limited capacity.
The main stage area of a virtual event where keynotes and main presentations are broadcast to all attendees.
A single-use, time-limited URL that allows an attendee to access an event without requiring a separate login. Used for seamless attendee onboarding.
An analytics report generated for an event, covering attendee data, engagement metrics, and other event statistics. Reports are available for download.
A label that can be applied to booths and other resources to categorize and filter them within an event.
A webhook subscription that sends real-time event data updates to a specified URL. Used to integrate RingCentral Events data with external systems.
An item on the event agenda/schedule, representing any time-blocked activity, session, or break during the event.
A reusable event template that pre-configures event settings, design, and structure for quick event creation.
A custom registration question associated with a ticket type, used to collect additional attendee information during registration.
The authentication method used by the RingCentral Events API. Client applications obtain access tokens using their client ID and client secret.
The subscription tier required to access the RingCentral Events External API. Lower-tier plans do not have API access.
The process of copying an existing event to create a new event with the same structure and settings.
The process of marking an attendee as having arrived and joined an event. Registration status changes to checked_in.
